title: Walsura chrysogyne
taxonomy: Walsura chrysogyne belongs to the family Meliaceae. This plant is a species of evergreen tree. It is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. Walsura chrysogyne is classified as a member of the genus Walsura in the Meliaceae family. The exact classification within the genus is Walsura chrysogyne.
uses: Walsura chrysogyne is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of various ailments. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which could aid in wound healing and the management of infections. intro: Walsura chrysogyne, also known as the 'Asoka' or 'Asogva', is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of Asia.
